zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de Holand II於大約1246年出生在英國藍卡爾武普 霍蘭時，他的父親Sir Thurstan de Holand26歲，母親de Kellet20歲。他於1272年與Elizabeth de Salmesbury在英國英格蘭藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1305年之前在英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯頓，埋葬在英國英格蘭藍卡爾普雷斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert de Holand II于大约1246年出生在英国藍卡爾武普 霍蘭时，他的父亲Sir Thurstan de Holand26岁，母亲de Kellet20岁。他于1272年与Elizabeth de Salmesbury在英国英格兰藍卡爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1305年之前在英国英格兰藍卡爾普雷斯頓，埋葬在英国英格兰藍卡爾普雷斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Holand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Holland
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de Holand II was born about 1246, in Up Holland, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir Thurstan de Holand, was 26 and his mother, de Kellet, was 20. He married Elizabeth de Salmesbury in 1272, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died before 1305, in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Preston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert de Holand II est né environ 1246, à Up Holland, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thurstan de Holand, avait 26 ans et sa mère, de Kellet, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Salmesbury en 1272, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 1305, à Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Holand IIが約1246年にイギリスランカシュアイアウップ ホーランドで生まれた時，父Sir Thurstan de Holandは26歳，母de Kelletは20歳であった。1272年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアでElizabeth de Salmesburyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1305年以前にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアプレストンで死去し，イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアプレストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Holand II(이)가 대략 1246년 영국 랭카실르 업 홀랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thurstan de Holand(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 de Kellet(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1272년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 Elizabeth de Salmesbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1305년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 플레스턴에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 플레스턴에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Salmesbury em 1272, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de Holand II родился около 1246 г. в местности уп холланд, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thurstan de Holand было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали de Kellet, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Salmesbury в 1272 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер перед 1305 г. в местности престон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности престон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.
